prompt: AI agent and agentic-workflow startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, amount, date, the wedge, and any traction/ARR. Cover horizontal agent platforms, agent infra, and notable vertical agents. Cite sources.
content:
**Key examples from 2025–early 2026 (through ~June 2026) that meet the $50M+ threshold in disclosed rounds, focused on AI agents/agentic workflows.**[[1]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/20/indian-vibe-coding-startup-emergent-raises-70m-at-300m-valuation-from-softbank-khosla-ventures/)[[2]](https://www.parloa.com/parloa-in-the-press/parloa-valued-at-3-billion-with-350m-series-d/)[[3]](https://decagon.ai/blog/series-d-announcement)

Information draws from funding announcements, company blogs, TechCrunch, and related reports. Note that exact dates can vary slightly across sources (often announced in Jan–May 2026), ARR/traction details are self-reported or estimated where available, and “wedge” refers to the core differentiation or market focus. Coverage prioritizes horizontal platforms, agent infrastructure, and notable verticals; not exhaustive, as many rounds emphasize enterprise AI agents or workflows.

### Horizontal Agent Platforms
- **Emergent**  
  **Amount/Date**: $70M Series B (Jan 2026; total raised ~$100M including prior ~$23M Series A in 2025). Valuation ~$300M post-money.  
  **Wedge**: “Vibe-coding”/agentic platform enabling non-technical users to describe ideas (“vibes”) and generate/deploy full-stack web/mobile apps via AI agents and workflows (handling backend, payments, deployment, etc.).  
  **Traction/ARR**: 5M+ users across 190+ countries; built 6M+ apps; ~$50M ARR (targeting $100M soon). Rapid scaling post-launch (7 months to Series B).[[1]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/20/indian-vibe-coding-startup-emergent-raises-70m-at-300m-valuation-from-softbank-khosla-ventures/)[[4]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/analytics-india-magazine_san-francisco-based-ai-startup-emergent-has-activity-7419337448188637184-vej3)

### Agent Infrastructure
- **Parallel (Parallel Web Systems)**  
  **Amount/Date**: $100M Series B (April 29, 2026) at $2B valuation (total raised $230M; prior $100M Series A in late 2025 at $740M valuation).  
  **Wedge**: Specialized web-search/retrieval infrastructure and APIs purpose-built for AI agents (deep research, source verification, real-time open-web access, tool APIs; SOC-2 certified). Positions itself as the “infrastructure layer for the web’s second user: AI agents.”  
  **Traction/ARR**: Strong investor conviction reflected in rapid valuation growth and participation from Sequoia (lead), Kleiner Perkins, Index, Khosla, etc. No public ARR disclosed in results, but category momentum for agent-specific retrieval/verification tools.[[5]](https://parallel.ai/blog/series-b)[[6]](https://parallel.ai/)

### Notable Vertical Agents
- **Decagon**  
  **Amount/Date**: $250M Series D (Jan 28, 2026); valuation tripled to $4.5B (total raised ~$481M+).  
  **Wedge**: Enterprise AI agents for customer support/concierge (handles complex multi-turn consultations across chat/email/voice; deep integrations with existing systems; proactive agents). Competes in contact-center automation.  
  **Traction/ARR**: $50M+ ARR; 100+ new global enterprise customers (examples: Avis Budget Group, Block, Deutsche Telekom); strong ROI focus in customer support automation.[[3]](https://decagon.ai/blog/series-d-announcement)[[7]](https://www.tryanalyze.ai/blog/fastest-growing-ai-companies)

- **Parloa**  
  **Amount/Date**: $350M Series D (Jan 15, 2026) at $3B valuation (total raised >$560M; prior unicorn round ~8 months earlier).  
  **Wedge**: Agentic AI platform for enterprise customer experience/service (autonomous AI agents elevating CX in contact centers and support workflows).  
  **Traction/ARR**: $50M+ ARR; major enterprise clients; rapid scaling with repeated large rounds from General Catalyst (lead), EQT, Altimeter, etc.[[2]](https://www.parloa.com/parloa-in-the-press/parloa-valued-at-3-billion-with-350m-series-d/)[[8]](https://www.facebook.com/groups/cto.platform/posts/2355907968188565/)

**Additional notes**: Other mentions in results (e.g., Rogo ~$75M Series C for financial/agentic workflows; Standard Intelligence $75M Series A) appear relevant but had fewer specifics on agent focus, exact dates, or traction in the searched sources. Broader agentic AI funding was robust in 2025 (~$2.9B across deals) and early 2026, with vertical customer support and horizontal/infra layers attracting significant capital.[[9]](https://newmarketpitch.com/blogs/news/agentic-ai-funding-trends) ARR figures are often reported at the time of raises and can evolve quickly. For the latest or more companies, targeted Crunchbase/TechCrunch searches or company announcements are recommended, as deal flow remained active.
